scenarios.title: Staging breach escalates to production telemetry exposure

source_evidence.quote_text:
## Cybersecurity

In November 2025 we detected unauthorized access to a staging environment containing
non-production customer telemetry. Forensics are ongoing. We have not determined whether
production systems or personally identifiable information were affected.

We are enhancing zero-trust network segmentation and MFA coverage for privileged accounts.

causal_chain: Attacker pivots from staging to shared IAM misconfiguration
Production customer telemetry exfiltrated for 3 weeks undetected
EU OEMs suspend connectivity features pending investigation
Regulatory fines and contractual indemnity claims accumulate
scenarios.missed_risk_rationale: Cyber disclosure is preliminary; production impact not ruled out.
mitigations: Segment staging from production identity plane

scenarios.title: Single-foundry ASIC cutoff after Taiwan disruption

source_evidence.quote_text:
A prolonged
outage could delay order fulfillment and financial close processes.

Foreign exchange fluctuations, particularly USD versus MYR and TWD, may reduce gross
margin when we cannot pass through cost increases.

causal_chain: Export controls tighten on advanced packaging in Taiwan
ACME cannot receive custom ASIC wafers for 6+ months
Controller production halts for automotive safety lines
Major OEMs invoke penalty clauses and switch to competitors
scenarios.missed_risk_rationale: 10-K discloses sole foundry dependency but understates cascade to customer penalties.

scenarios.title: SEC revenue recognition investigation triggers restatement spiral

source_evidence.quote_text:
## Legal Proceedings

The SEC is investigating our revenue recognition practices for multi-year service bundles
sold in 2023–2024. We cannot predict timing or outcome; adverse findings could require
restatement and trigger customer contract renegotiations.

A class action alleging misleading statements about supply chain resilience was filed
in the Northern District of California in January 2026.

causal_chain: SEC enforcement concludes misallocated multi-year bundle revenue
Financial restatement reduces reported EBITDA below covenant
Revolver covenant breach accelerates debt repayment demands
Customers renegotiate contracts citing material adverse change
scenarios.missed_risk_rationale: Legal section notes investigation but not covenant linkage in MD&A.

scenarios.title: EU machinery regulation blocks 19% revenue base

source_evidence.quote_text:
## Regulatory Exposure

New EU machinery regulation (EU) 2023/1230 requires additional functional safety
documentation by March 2027. Non-compliance could block shipments to European OEMs
representing 19% of revenue.

U.S. CHIPS Act incentives require domestic content thresholds we may not meet without
requalifying suppliers.

causal_chain: Functional safety documentation misses March 2027 deadline
EU shipments halted for non-compliant controller families
European OEM programs defer volume ramps
Fixed cost base absorbs revenue gap; liquidity tightens
scenarios.missed_risk_rationale: Regulatory section cites deadline but not revenue-at-risk quantification.
mitigations: Accelerate safety file submissions per product line

scenarios.title: Japanese sole-source passive shortage ends legacy platform support

source_evidence.quote_text:
## Supply Chain Dependencies

Critical passive components and power modules are sole-sourced from two Japanese vendors.
Lead times extended from 14 to 38 weeks during fiscal 2025. We hold 90 days of safety
stock for tier-1 SKUs but not for legacy platforms scheduled for end-of-life in 2026.

causal_chain: 38-week lead times extend to allocation rationing
Legacy platforms lack safety stock coverage
Installed base customers face unplanned downtime
Service revenue collapses; replacement parts litigation rises
scenarios.missed_risk_rationale: Supply chain notes legacy EOL but not service-revenue exposure.
